checks.detail: instruction.md, task.toml, environment/Dockerfile, and tests/test.sh all exist.
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml are all present.
allow_internet=false, agent.timeout_sec=7200, verifier.timeout_sec=600, build_timeout_sec=600, agent.user=agent.
git and tmux are installed via apt-get in the Dockerfile.
tests/ contains extra assets (compute_reward.py, harness.py, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, visible_cases/) that should live in environment/tests/ and be bundled at build time.
reasons: compute_reward.py present in tests/
harness.py present in tests/
hidden_cases/ directory in tests/
perf_data/ directory in tests/
visible_cases/ directory in tests/

checks.detail: No stray files at task root after ignoring gitignored and standard-ignored entries.

notes: Move compute_reward.py, harness.py, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, and visible_cases/ from tests/ into environment/tests/ and COPY them into /tests at build time so tests/ contains only test.sh.

checks.detail: task.toml sets [agent] user = "agent" for non-root agent execution.
Dockerfile creates non-root agent, locks /tests, hands /app and /solution to agent, and installs strace.
reasons: present: useradd --create-home agent
present: chown -R agent:agent /app
present: mkdir /solution + chown agent:agent /solution
present: mkdir /tests + chmod 700 /tests
present: strace installed (apt-get) for verifier auditing
present: cmark reference installed in /opt/verifier_tools as root-only (chmod -R go-rwx)
note: uid not explicitly pinned to 1000 but useradd on python:3.11-slim assigns 1000 by default; test.sh chmod go-rx pattern is consistent with non-root agent

checks.detail: test.sh locks /logs/verifier first then wipes, re-locks /tests, runs all agent-code phases as `su agent` inside strace -f with timeout, and computes reward outside strace.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier BEFORE rm -rf (lines 54-55)
present: re-lock /tests (chmod 700 + go-rx on subdirs) after Harbor upload resets perms
present: env sanitisation (unsets LD_PRELOAD, LD_LIBRARY_PATH, PYTHONPATH, CC, CFLAGS, etc.)
present: exit 0 always (fail_with exits 0; final exit 0)
present: fail_with writes 0.0 fallback via compute_reward.py --fail
present: build, correctness run, and perf runs all wrapped in `strace -f -e trace=clone,fork,vfork,execve,openat` + `timeout` + `su agent -s /bin/bash -c "env -i ..."`
present: reward_tamper_in() greps strace logs for openat write of reward.{txt,json} after every strace'd phase
present: converter_execve_in() greps for cmark/pandoc/python/node/perl/ruby execve in md2html runs
present: pkill -9 -u agent before verification to clear pre-existing agent procs
present: compute_reward.py runs OUTSIDE strace as root and does not import or read /app
present: no chown root:root on /logs paths (only relax_reward_perms widens reads after scoring)
present: oracle-marker gate uses random token from [solution.env], unreadable by agent

checks.detail: runs_agent_code — verifier compiles the agent's C sources and executes the resulting md2html binary, so strace + su agent are mandatory and present.
reasons: verifier invokes make/gcc on /app/workspace/*.c
verifier execs the produced /app/workspace/md2html for correctness and perf phases
strace -f wraps every agent-code execution path; scoring (compute_reward.py) runs outside strace

checks.detail: No Python/Node/Rust dependency manifests; only apt packages and an unpinned cmark git clone.
Task is pure C with only stdlib Python in tests; no pip/uv usage.
task.toml has no docker_image field (acceptable during development; required before final delivery).
reasons: docker_image not set in [environment]

notes: cmark is cloned with --depth 1 from main without a pinned tag/commit; pin a specific release before final delivery for reproducibility.
Set docker_image in task.toml before final delivery.

checks.detail: Deliverable, interface, compilation command, and CommonMark scope are unambiguous.
Explicit '2 hours' time limit at the end.
Mostly clear but reads more like a spec than a colleague handoff, with formal section headers and rule lists.
reasons: Sections like 'Implementation Rules' and 'Goals' lean spec-like rather than conversational.

checks.detail: 107 lines, slightly over the 100-line target.
reasons: Consider moving the visible-test bash snippet or the 'Tricky areas' bullets into a workspace README.

checks.detail: No mention of scorer, verifier, reward, or evaluation; 'tests' refers only to the visible CommonMark corpus.
make, gcc, python3, and the starter Makefile/md2html.c are all mentioned.

notes: Trim to <=100 lines by shortening 'Tricky areas' or relocating the visible-test loop into a workspace README.
Soften tone: replace 'What You Must Implement' / 'Implementation Rules' headings with a brief colleague-handoff paragraph.

checks.detail: Starter md2html outputs empty string, fails all cases; correctness=0 gates performance to 0, composite=0.0.

Reward dir locked 700 first thing, /tests re-locked 700, all agent code runs as agent user under strace with timeouts, output files pre-created in root-owned 0755 dirs preventing unlink/symlink swaps.
Build, correctness runner, and performance runs all execute via `su agent -s /bin/bash -c ...` with env -i; no agent code runs as root.
Build, correctness, and one perf run per file are wrapped in `strace -f` and logs are checked for reward-file openat writes and external-converter execve before scoring.
compute_reward.py runs as `python3 -ISs` outside strace, reads only CLI counters and a root-written failed-cases file in /logs/verifier; never imports from /app.
fail_with() always writes reward 0.0 and exits 0; strace/timed subprocesses are wrapped in `timeout`; no `set -e` so errors don't abort early.
Perf is multiplied by correctness_rate, so any incorrect/regressing solution collapses both subscores toward 0.
Correctness compares HTML output to CommonMark reference and perf is wall-clock vs cmark, both measure the deliverable not a specific algorithm.
Two dimensions (correctness, performance) with performance gated multiplicatively on correctness so neither can be maxed in isolation while ignoring the other.
Empty/constant/copy-input outputs fail nearly all 99 cases, dropping correctness to ~0 and dragging the gated perf term to ~0 as well.
compute_reward.py runs outside strace as root, reads only CLI counters and a root-written failed-cases file, never imports or executes agent code.
Both success and --fail paths write reward.json with score, subscores list, and additional_data, and reward.txt with a single float.

notes: Consider adding 1-2 more perf inputs (e.g. flat long list, deep block-quote nesting) so a single pathological file does not dominate the perf subscore.

checks.detail: Instruction mentions correctness and speed but does not disclose that performance is 50% of the score or that the speed gate compares against cmark with a 5x factor.
reasons: Performance weight (50%) and cmark-relative 5x speed ratio are not inferable from instruction.md

checks.detail: CommonMark spec is linked and tricky terms (loose/tight, lazy continuation, list-item indentation) are defined in instruction.md.
Dockerfile installs gcc/make/build-essential and workspace ships Makefile + md2html.c stub matching the documented build commands.
solution/solve.sh exists (2359 bytes) and installs a token-gated cmark wrapper as the reference oracle.
Hidden cases cover the same three CommonMark sections (Lists, List items, Block quotes) declared in instruction.md, as structural variants.

notes: Consider disclosing in instruction.md that scoring weighs correctness and performance equally, and that performance is measured relative to a reference C CommonMark implementation — this helps the agent prioritize iteration time appropriately.

checks.detail: Slim Python base, apt cache cleaned, cmake purged after cmark build; every remaining package has a load-bearing use in test.sh or as a Harbor hard rule.
solution/ contains only solve.sh, which writes the oracle marker and installs a cmark-wrapper reference.
Core orchestration is sound, but several blocks are redundant given the single-container, fresh-per-trial isolation model.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 + rm -rf on /logs/verifier (mandatory)
useful: re-locking /tests after Harbor's verify-phase upload resets it to 0755
useful: strace-wrapped build and run as agent user (double defense)
useful: untraced timed perf runs + separate traced run for tripwires (avoids ptrace skewing timing)
useful: env -i + sanitised PATH for the agent shell
redundant: pkill/pgrep block at lines 146-153 (each trial is a fresh container; no cross-trial agent process can exist)
redundant: workspace_snapshot at lines 158-164 is written but never read by any downstream step
redundant: find /tests -type f -name '*.sh' -exec chmod +x at line 72 (no .sh files exist under /tests besides test.sh itself, which is already executing)
redundant: separate make.log and gcc.log files when both are short build logs (could fold into one log)

checks.detail: .gitignore covers .env and jobs/; no individual file exceeds 30MB (largest is perf_nested_lists.md at 1.7MB).

tests/ at the task root holds 2.1MB of assets (visible_cases, hidden_cases, perf_data, harness.py, compute_reward.py) that per Harbor spec belong in environment/tests/ and should be baked into the image — tests/ should contain only test.sh.

notes: Move tests/{visible_cases,hidden_cases,perf_data,harness.py,compute_reward.py} into environment/tests/ and add COPY environment/tests/ /tests/ in the Dockerfile (followed by chmod -R 700 /tests). Leave only test.sh at the task root.
Drop the pkill, workspace_snapshot, and stray chmod +x blocks from test.sh; they add log noise without closing any vector that user separation + the /logs/verifier lockdown does not already close.
Add a minimal environment/.dockerignore (at least: jobs/, results/, qa-logs/, .git, *.bak).

instruction_quality.task_name: CommonMark container blocks converter in C
instruction_quality.task_summary: Implement a from-scratch C program that converts CommonMark Markdown (lists and block quotes) to HTML via stdin/stdout. The binary builds with make at /app/workspace/md2html and must match reference HTML on 73 visible plus held-out cases while staying fast.

measures: /tests re-locked to 700 after Harbor's upload-phase reset
Output staging dirs root-owned 0755 with pre-created 0666 files (no unlink/symlink swap)
FD-pinned O_NOFOLLOW reads of agent output, reject non-regular/hardlinked files
Agent runs as `agent` (uid 1000); verifier runs as root
Every build/run of agent code uses `su agent -s /bin/bash -c` with `env -i` to strip inherited state
cmark reference installed in /opt/verifier_tools with chmod -R go-rwx
strace -f wraps build, correctness, and per-perf-file runs
Reward-file openat-write tripwire on every strace log
External-converter execve tripwire on correctness + perf strace logs (skipped only on verified oracle marker)
pkill -u agent before verification to clear leftover background processes
Static source scan for Markdown library #includes, exec-family of external converters, and wrapper scripts
ELF file-type check rejects shell/python wrappers
Random 16-hex oracle marker token gates anti-cheat bypass; token never exposed to agent (lives in root-only /tests/test.sh and [solution.env])
compute_reward.py and harness.py invoked with `python3 -ISs` (no site, no env)
Performance gated by correctness (perf_rate *= correctness_rate) so fast-empty binaries get 0

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Timed perf runs are untraced (strace overhead would skew timing); a converter could in principle behave differently when not traced.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Reward file is chmod 700 root-owned during these runs, /tests is locked, and the agent uid has no system-write surface; the one traced perf run per file would catch external-converter execve and an attacker has no way to alter scoring from an untraced agent-uid run.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: /app/workspace is agent-writable; agent could plant conftest-style or .pth files.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Verifier never imports from /app; harness.py and compute_reward.py run with `python3 -ISs` and read only /tests + root-written counters.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: /logs/agent is 777 throughout — agent can tamper with its own traces.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Documented Harbor limitation; agent self-trace tampering does not affect reward computation.

reward_design.formula: composite = 0.5 * correctness_rate + 0.5 * (perf_raw * correctness_rate); per-file perf = min(1.0, (cmark_median * 5.0) / agent_median); correctness_rate = (visible_pass + hidden_pass) / total
reward_design.formula_explanation: Weighted sum of correctness and performance with performance multiplicatively gated by correctness so fast-but-wrong converters cannot harvest half the score.

components.name: correctness
components.metric: fraction of visible+hidden CommonMark cases whose HTML output exactly matches the reference

components.name: performance
components.metric: mean of per-file min(1, 5 * cmark_median / agent_median) across perf corpus

dimension_analysis.name: correctness
dimension_analysis.notes: 99 cases (73 visible + 26 hidden) covering lists, block quotes, loose/tight, lazy continuation, nesting; spec-exact match required.

dimension_analysis.name: performance
dimension_analysis.notes: Only one perf file (perf_nested_lists.md) so perf signal is noisier than ideal, but 5x cmark threshold is generous and gating by correctness prevents standalone gaming.

redundant_items.item: 73 JSON case files duplicated byte-for-byte under environment/test-suite/
redundant_items.why: visible_cases/ exists only because test.sh reads it from /tests/visible_cases at runtime; if these assets lived in environment/tests/ they would be COPYed into the image once and never re-uploaded per trial, eliminating the duplication

redundant_items.item: 26 hidden case JSONs sitting in the task root
redundant_items.why: per Harbor spec, tests/ at the task root should contain only test.sh; hidden_cases belongs in environment/tests/ so it ships inside the Docker image (faster uploads, leaner repo, cleaner upload-merge surface)
static benchmark data should be baked into the image via COPY environment/tests/ /tests/ rather than re-shipped through docker compose cp on every verifier upload

redundant_items.item: Python verifier modules sitting in the task root
redundant_items.why: code that the verifier executes should be COPYed into the image at build time (environment/tests/ -> /tests/); only test.sh needs to live at the task root for Harbor's upload step

redundant_items.item: pkill/pgrep cross-phase isolation block (lines 146-153) including the fail_with branch
redundant_items.why: each trial runs in a fresh container with PID 1 = sleep infinity; no agent process can pre-exist before test.sh runs, and the /logs/verifier chmod 700 already cuts off any agent background process from writing the reward file

the snapshot is copied to /logs/verifier/workspace_snapshot but never consumed by any later step; if forensic preservation is the goal, Harbor's artifact collection already grabs /app/workspace per job.yaml
no .sh file exists under /tests besides test.sh itself, which is already executing; the comment even concedes this is a Modal/Daytona safety net but the task is configured for local Docker
no exclusions means anything added to environment/ later (build artifacts, editor files) will land in the build context — a one-line .dockerignore is cheap insurance
